<!--
NEW PAPER — ISSC internal review.
Paste your completed cover-page text below this checklist (from Publications/<your-branch>.md).
The review happens IN THIS PULL REQUEST. Do not open an Issue for the review itself.
-->

## 1 · Author checklist — before requesting review
- [ ] Created a new branch off `main`, named for the publication
- [ ] Added my completed cover page to `Publications/<name>.md` on that branch and committed it
- [ ] Pasted the cover-page text into this PR description (below)
- [ ] Included a link to where the publication can be found
- [ ] Filled in **Category**, **Class**, and **Timeline stream**
- [ ] Listed **author contact(s)** (1–3, at least one ISSC member)
- [ ] Wrote the **Detailed description** as an enumerated section outline
- [ ] Filled in the **Data rights** section (if the work touches real data)
- [ ] Assignees = all authors (add the Paper Handler once assigned)
- [ ] Opened this as a **Draft** PR, with **no reviewers** assigned yet

## 2 · Paper Handler — opening the First Review
- [ ] Procedural compliance checked; Category / Class / Timeline confirmed or adjusted
- [ ] Applied labels: category · class · timeline · `status: first review`
- [ ] Converted **Draft → Ready for review**
- [ ] Added any author-requested expert reviewers to **Reviewers**
- [ ] Announced to the ISSC by email (link to this PR + abstract + link to the publication)
- [ ] First Review deadline set per the stream

## 3 · First Review — for reviewers (any ISSC member)
Post comments in one of two ways:
- On a **line under the relevant section** in the **Files changed** tab, then **Start a review**; or
- As a comment / reaction under **Conversation**.

Conventions:
- Prefix a comment with **`Request:`** if it is required for the publication's validity. Everything else is a **suggestion**.
- Use 👍 to **upvote** comments — more-upvoted comments are higher priority to resolve.
- You are reviewing *readiness for publication as an ISSC product*. The team's premise has already been developed and does not itself merit criticism.

## 4 · First Review — for authors
- [ ] Responded to **every** comment in-thread
- [ ] Offered co-authorship to reviewer(s) whose feedback significantly improved the work
- [ ] Uploaded a revised version at the stated link
- [ ] Notified the Paper Handler

## 5 · Paper Handler — closing the First Review
- [ ] Reminded reviewers to re-review and mark their comments resolved
- [ ] The reviewer who first posted a comment resolves it when satisfied
- [ ] Adjudicated anything still unresolved (resolved vs. carry to Follow-up)
- [ ] For any un-adopted `Request:` — either re-classified it to a suggestion (with the authors' justification) or the change was made

## 6 · Follow-up Review (repeat steps 3–5 against the revised version)
- [ ] Runs only if `Request:` comments remain; may repeat **once more** for newly raised Requests the Paper Handler agrees are needed
- [ ] Timeline kept consistent with the original stream

## 7 · Conclusion
- [ ] Paper Handler **releases** and makes a recommendation to the **PubCom Chair**
- [ ] **Chair records the final decision**
- [ ] Final version adds ISSC attribution — "The LSST Informatics and Statistics Scientific Collaboration" after the author list (Mandatory / Encouraged), or an acknowledgement of the ISSC review (Optional)
- [ ] Final decision + final version posted here; label → `status: concluded`; PR merged
